So how does Gevura, how is the goal of Gevura space?
So Gevura is the idea that I respect the other person's individuality,
and I don't give them value based on how similar they are to me.
Chesed says, oh, if you're like me, then you have value.
Gavurah says, you can have value even if you're not like me.
Which is, to me, the most important thing in any relationship.
If I can respect the other, it's easy to love.
It's harder to respect.
You know, if somebody loves someone, guess what?
They can love someone else also.
That love is not exclusive.
But if you respect them, then you're going to respect them.
There's a difference there.
If you respect the other, now you've created boundaries around that relationship.
If you love them, sure, but you can also love someone else simultaneously.
Respect is a little bit different.
So respect is, and that's gavur.
Gavur is creating those boundaries.
